prose:
Method–provenance block
Property: Atom-condensed Fukui functions (f+, f−, f0) and dual descriptor; identification of most electrophilic (max f+) and most nucleophilic (max f−) atomic sites for aniline.
Level of theory: DFT tier `standard` → B3LYP/def2-TZVP with RI (density fitting) in PySCF.
Geometry provenance: single–point Fukui analysis on the fixed experimental geometry supplied by the user (/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz); no re-optimization was performed (notation: fukui//given geometry).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).
Charge and multiplicity: neutral molecule, charge 0, multiplicity 1; N±1 states were modeled as doublets (cation: +1, multiplicity 2; anion: −1, multiplicity 2).
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (PySCF backend, Mulliken population analysis for charges).
Convergence & caveats: SCF cycles for N, N+1, and N−1 states all converged (integrity.status = "ok", trustworthy = true). Warnings returned by the engine are reproduced verbatim below.

Key numerical result (headline)
Most electrophilic site (largest f+): atom 3 (C) with f+ = 2.2975867 (dimensionless).
Most nucleophilic site (largest f−): atom 1 (N) with f− = 0.20452359 (dimensionless).
